key_insight: The paper replaces explicit differentiation with zeroth-order Monte Carlo estimators to learn PDE solutions and their derivatives in a fully model-free way.
engineering_relevance: This enables scalable, black-box PDE solving for high-dimensional scientific ML and reinforcement learning settings where system dynamics are only accessible through simulation.
method: Use perturbed Monte Carlo trajectories to build zeroth-order derivative estimators for gradients and Hessians.
Train solution, gradient, and Hessian networks from pointwise function evaluations rather than automatic differentiation.
Prove a non-asymptotic error bound that separates discretization, approximation, statistical, and ZOD-bias terms.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments showing competitive performance in moderate and high dimensions, alongside a statistical learning analysis and sample-complexity results.
watch_item: ZOD estimators introduce bias-variance tradeoffs, so performance depends on perturbation design, simulation quality, and the contraction assumption.
paper_title: A Zeroth-Order Deep Learning Method for Fully Nonlinear Parabolic Partial Differential Equations with Unknown Coefficients
